steps.title: Identify the structure
steps.content: The text introduces a list of exoplanets, each described with its distance and name, separated by semicolons (the second item already starts with 'at 352 light-years away, the super Earth Kepler-102 f'). The blank ends an independent clause and starts the list.

steps.title: Apply the rule
steps.content:
A colon introduces the list after a complete clause. 'Earth: at 41 light-years away, the super Earth TRAPPIST-1 b' parallels 'at 352 light-years away, the super Earth Kepler-102 f.' Choices A and D wrongly attach the distance phrase to 'Earth' with a comma; C inverts the punctuation.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
